# Neuroscience Bulletin

**Caution: some of the codes are generated by Kimi K2.5 model and other LLMs, which may include some hallucination.**

Maintainers dedicate Neuroscience Bulletin repo to be a WeChat public account that looks up most recent neuroscience articles and shares them with you. We provide a complete end-to-end pipeline:
- 🕷️ Crawl titles, abstracts and metadata from 15+ top neuroscience-related journals and preprint servers
- 🔍 Enrich papers with abstracts, affiliation information using Europe PMC and ROR (Research Organization Registry)
- 💾 Store all processed data in SQLite database for easy querying and analysis
- 🤖 Summarize papers and generate weekly research reports using multiple LLMs (Qwen3.5-plus, Doubao-Seed-1.8_TPM, DeepSeek-V3.2)
- 📊 Generate data visualizations of global research output, country distribution and paper statistics

We have published 6 issues so far, all content is available on our Zhihu column "Neuroscience Bulletin": https://www.zhihu.com/column/c_2016331747525157941
Latest issue (Issue 6): https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/2028897146196206750

This repository contains the code and data of the Neuroscience Bulletin project. We wish to make the project open-source and accessible to everyone. If you have any questions or suggestions, please feel free to contact us.

---

## 🚀 Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Core crawler dependencies
pip install requests beautifulsoup4 jsonlines python-dateutil tqdm selenium

# Enrichment & database dependencies
pip install pandas numpy sqlite3

# LLM processing dependencies
pip install openai tiktoken

# Visualization dependencies
pip install plotly matplotlib seaborn
```

### Usage

```bash
# Fetch articles from all sources (default last 7 days)
python src/main.py

# Fetch only from arXiv and bioRxiv (preprints)
python src/main.py --arxiv-only --biorxiv-only

# Fetch from last 14 days instead of 7 (applies to all sources)
python src/main.py --days 14

# Enrich papers with ROR affiliation data
python src/batch_enrich_authors.py

# Build SQLite database from processed papers
python sql_scripts/build_sqlite.py

# Generate LLM summaries and reports
python LLM_eval/main.py

# Generate data visualizations
python visualize/main.py
```

See `python src/main.py --help` for all crawler options.

---

## 📚 Supported Sources

### Preprint Servers ✅

| Source | Status | Description |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| **arXiv** | ✅ Supported | q-bio.NC, q-bio.TO, q-bio.MN categories |
| **bioRxiv** | ✅ Supported | All neuroscience-related preprints |

**Note on arXiv:** Fetches from three core neuroscience categories:
- **q-bio.NC** - Neurons and Cognition (primary neuroscience)
- **q-bio.TO** - Tissues and Organs (neural tissues, brain organoids)
- **q-bio.MN** - Molecular Networks (molecular neuroscience)

### Springer Nature Journals ✅

| Journal | Status |
|---------|--------|
| Nature | ✅ |
| Nature Biomedical Engineering | ✅ |
| Nature Methods | ✅ |
| Nature Neuroscience | ✅ |
| Nature Reviews Neuroscience | ✅ |
| Nature Human Behaviour | ✅ |

### Other Journals ✅

| Journal | Status | Method |
|---------|--------|--------|
| **Science** | ✅ Supported | List pages + Europe PMC enrichment |
| **Science Advances** | ✅ Supported | TOC page + Selenium (date filtered) |
| **PNAS** | ✅ Supported | PubMed API |
| **Cell Press** | ✅ Supported | Selenium + Europe PMC enrichment |
| **Nature Communications** | ✅ Supported | Subject pages (Biological/Health Sciences) |
| **Brain** | ✅ Supported | PubMed API |
| **eLife** | ✅ Supported | PubMed API |
| **Journal of Neurophysiology** | ✅ Supported | PubMed (primary) + Europe PMC (supplementary) |
| **Journal of Neuroscience** | ✅ Supported | PubMed API (filters Journal Club articles by default) |
| **Journal of Cognitive Neuroscience** | ✅ Supported | PubMed API |
| **Journal of Vision** | ✅ Supported | PubMed API |
| **Scientific Reports** | ✅ Supported | PubMed API |

**Note on Science:** Uses a smart enrichment strategy:
1. Fetch basic info from Science list pages (no captcha)
2. Enrich with Europe PMC via DOI (abstract, PMID, etc.)
3. Fallback to preprint servers (bioRxiv/arXiv) if not in Europe PMC
4. Keep original data if nothing is found

---

## 🔬 Data Enrichment

### Metadata Enrichment

| Service | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| **Europe PMC** | Primary enrichment source (PubMed abstracts, PMC full-text, PMID/PMCID) |
| **Preprint Fallback** | Search bioRxiv/arXiv for preprint versions of published papers |

### ROR Affiliation Enrichment ✅

We use the Research Organization Registry (ROR) to normalize author affiliation information:
- Normalize institution names to standard ROR identifiers
- Extract country/region information for all authors
- Generate statistics on global research output distribution
- Support for batch processing of large paper datasets

---

## 💾 SQLite Database

All processed papers are stored in a SQLite database for efficient querying and analysis:
- Schema supports all paper metadata, enrichment information and affiliation data
- Fast querying by date, source, journal, country, and keywords
- Integrated with visualization and LLM processing modules
- Automatic deduplication of papers from multiple sources

---

## 🤖 LLM Analysis & Report Generation

We use multiple large language models to process collected papers:
- **Supported Models**: Qwen3.5-plus, Doubao-Seed-1.8_TPM, DeepSeek-V3.2
- **Core Functions**:
  - Summarize paper abstracts into concise, easy-to-understand highlights
  - Categorize papers by research topic (cognitive neuroscience, molecular neuroscience, clinical neuroscience, etc.)
  - Generate weekly research reports for WeChat public account and Zhihu
  - Support custom prompt templates for different use cases
- **Outputs**: Structured JSON analysis results, formatted Markdown reports

---

## 📊 Data Visualization

Built-in visualization module for analyzing research trends:
- **Global Heatmap**: Visualize research output distribution by country/region
- **Country Distribution Pie Chart**: Show proportion of papers from different countries
- **Paper Score Histogram**: Statistical distribution of paper influence metrics
- **Output Formats**: Static PNG images, interactive HTML charts
- All visualizations are automatically generated as part of the weekly pipeline

---

## 📁 Project Structure

```
.
├── src/                          # Core crawler and enrichment code
│   ├── main.py                  # Main crawler entry point
│   ├── main_beta.py             # Beta version of crawler
│   ├── crawler_*.py             # Individual crawlers for 15+ journals/preprint servers
│   ├── enrich_papers.py         # First-level paper enrichment (abstracts, PMID/PMCID from Europe PMC)
│   ├── enrich_authors.py        # Author affiliation enrichment with ROR data
│   ├── batch_enrich_authors.py  # Batch processing for ROR enrichment
│   ├── ror_refine_batch.py      # ROR data refinement
│   ├── supp_func.py             # Support functions
│   └── utils.py                 # Utility functions
├── LLM_eval/                     # LLM analysis and report generation module
│   ├── main.py                  # LLM processing entry point
│   ├── call_API.py              # LLM API clients for Qwen, Doubao, DeepSeek
│   ├── Summary.py               # Paper summarization logic
│   ├── Summary_wechat.py        # WeChat public account report generation
│   ├── StructuredPrompt.py      # Prompt templates for LLM
│   ├── util.py                  # LLM utilities
│   └── util_enriched.py         # Enriched data processing utilities
├── visualize/                    # Data visualization module
│   ├── main.py                  # Visualization entry point
│   ├── dbapi.py                 # Database API for visualization
│   ├── global_heatmap.py        # Global research output heatmap generation
│   └── vis_stat.py              # Statistical charts (histograms, pie charts)
├── sql_scripts/                  # SQLite database scripts
│   ├── build_sqlite.py          # Database construction script
│   ├── schema.sql               # Database schema
│   └── sqlfuncs.py              # Database utility functions
├── data/                         # Static data files
│   ├── ROR*.json                # Research Organization Registry data for affiliation normalization
│   ├── *country*.json           # Country/region normalization mappings
│   ├── literature.db            # SQLite database storing all processed papers
│   └── normalize_country.py     # Country normalization script
├── getfiles/                     # Crawled and processed paper data
│   ├── all_papers_YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl              # Raw crawled papers
│   ├── all_papers_YYYY-MM-DD_enriched.jsonl     # Papers with abstract/metadata enrichment
│   └── all_papers_YYYY-MM-DD_enriched_ror_refined.jsonl  # Papers with full ROR affiliation enrichment
├── LLM_Results/                  # LLM output and generated reports
│   ├── LLM_results_*.json       # Raw LLM analysis results
│   ├── report_*.md              # Generated weekly research reports
│   └── report_*_wechat.md       # WeChat public account formatted reports
├── Imgs/                         # Visualization outputs
│   ├── visulize_img/            # Generated charts (heatmaps, pie charts, histograms in PNG/HTML format)
│   └── *.png                    # Weekly report cover images
├── bashScripts/                  # Build and pipeline scripts
│   ├── build_ror.*              # ROR data build scripts
│   ├── build_sql.*              # Database build scripts
│   └── sql_pipeline.bat         # Full data pipeline script
├── logs/                         # Log files
├── .gitignore
├── LICENSE
├── README.md                    # This file
└── ScriptOnColabNoScience.ipynb  # Colab notebook for running the pipeline
```

---

## 🔧 Development

### Testing Individual Crawlers

```bash
# Test arXiv crawler
python src/crawler_arxiv.py

# Test bioRxiv crawler
python src/crawler_biorxiv.py
```

### Adding a New Journal

1. Check if the journal provides an API or RSS feed
2. Create a new crawler file in `src/crawler_<journal>.py`
3. Implement the following interface:
   - `fetch_papers()`: Returns list of paper dicts with keys: `title`, `authors`, `date`, `url`, `abstract`
   - `save_papers()`: Saves to JSONL file
4. Add the crawler to `src/main.py`

---

## 📖 Data Format

Articles are saved in JSONL format with the following fields (enriched versions have additional fields):

```json
{
  "type": "Article",
  "title": "Paper title",
  "authors": ["Author 1", "Author 2", "..."],
  "affiliations": ["Institution 1, Country 1", "..."],
  "ror_ids": ["https://ror.org/xxxxxx", "..."],
  "countries": ["Country 1", "..."],
  "date": "DD MMM YYYY",
  "url": "https://...",
  "abstract": "Paper abstract...",
  "source": "arXiv|bioRxiv|Nature|...",
  "pdf_url": "https://... (optional)",
  "doi": "10.xxxx/... (optional)",
  "pmid": "xxxxxx (optional)",
  "pmcid": "PMCxxxxxx (optional)"
}
```

---

## ⚠️ Notes

### Rate Limiting

- **arXiv**: No strict rate limits, but please be respectful (1s delay between requests)
- **bioRxiv**: No rate limits documented
- **Nature/Science**: May require delays and user-agent rotation
- **LLM APIs**: Follow rate limits for respective model providers

### Anti-Bot Protection

Some publishers (Science, Cell/Elsevier) use Cloudflare or similar protection. Our approach:

1. **Preferred**: Use official APIs when available (arXiv, bioRxiv, PubMed)
2. **Alternative**: Use PubMed/Europe PMC for journal articles
3. **Last resort**: Selenium with manual captcha solving
